words.hint: Doctor — 'ir al médico' = to go to the doctor. Feminine: 'la médica'. Also 'el doctor / la doctora'. The person who treats illness.
Nurse — 'la enfermera le tomó la temperatura' = the nurse took his temperature. Feminine: 'la enfermera'. Essential in hospitals and clinics.
Hospital — 'llevar a alguien al hospital' = to take someone to the hospital. 'hospital público/privado' = public/private hospital.
Clinic — smaller and more specialized than a hospital. 'clínica dental' = dental clinic; 'clínica privada' = private clinic.
Pharmacy — 'comprar medicamentos en la farmacia' = to buy medicine at the pharmacy. Look for the green cross sign ('la cruz verde') in Spain and Latin America.
Prescription — 'necesito una receta médica' = I need a prescription. Same word as 'recipe' — context tells you which it is.
Medication — 'tomar el medicamento dos veces al día' = to take the medication twice a day. Also 'medicina' or 'fármaco' (more technical).
Pill — 'tomar una pastilla para el dolor' = to take a pill for the pain. Also 'comprimido' or 'tableta'. 'pastilla de jabón' = bar of soap (unrelated).
Injection — 'poner una inyección' = to give an injection. 'tengo miedo de las inyecciones' = I'm afraid of injections. Also 'vacuna' for a vaccine shot.
Vaccine — 'vacunarse contra la gripe' = to get vaccinated against the flu. 'vacuna obligatoria' = mandatory vaccine. From the Latin 'vacca' (cow — origin of first vaccine).
Surgery — 'necesita una cirugía de corazón' = he needs heart surgery. 'cirugía de urgencia' = emergency surgery. Also 'operación'.
Operation — 'recuperarse de una operación' = to recover from an operation. Synonymous with 'cirugía'. Also means 'operation' in a business/military sense.
Diagnosis — 'el médico dio su diagnóstico' = the doctor gave his diagnosis. From Greek 'diagnōsis' = knowledge through. 'diagnóstico precoz' = early diagnosis.
Symptom — '¿qué síntomas tienes?' = what symptoms do you have? 'síntomas de la gripe' = flu symptoms. Key vocabulary at a doctor's appointment.
Fever — 'tengo fiebre' = I have a fever. 'temperatura de 38 grados' = 38-degree temperature. Common symptom of infection. 'bajar la fiebre' = to bring down the fever.
Cough — 'tengo mucha tos' = I have a bad cough. 'jarabe para la tos' = cough syrup. Verb: 'toser' = to cough.
Cold — 'tengo un resfriado' = I have a cold. Also called 'el catarro'. 'estar resfriado' = to have a cold. More mild than 'la gripe'.
words.imageQuery: person with cold runny nose tissues

words.hint: Flu — 'tengo gripe y me duele todo' = I have the flu and everything hurts. More severe than 'resfriado'. 'vacuna contra la gripe' = flu vaccine.
Allergy — 'soy alérgico al gluten' = I'm allergic to gluten. 'alergia al polvo/al polen' = dust/pollen allergy. 'tengo una alergia alimentaria' = I have a food allergy.
Asthma — note: feminine noun but uses 'el' (like 'el agua'). 'el inhalador para el asma' = the asthma inhaler. A chronic breathing condition.
Diabetes — 'la diabetes tipo 2' = type 2 diabetes. 'controlar el azúcar en sangre' = to monitor blood sugar. A very common chronic disease.
words.imageQuery: diabetes blood sugar test glucose meter

words.hint: Hypertension — 'la hipertensión es la tensión arterial alta' = hypertension is high blood pressure. A silent but serious risk factor for heart disease.
Infection — 'la herida tiene una infección' = the wound has an infection. 'infección bacteriana/viral' = bacterial/viral infection. Requires antibiotic or antiviral treatment.
Wound — 'limpiar la herida' = to clean the wound. 'herida leve/grave' = minor/serious injury. 'estar herido' = to be wounded/injured.
Burn — 'quemadura de primer grado' = first-degree burn. 'quemarse con el horno' = to burn oneself on the oven. Cool immediately with cold water.
Fracture — 'tiene una fractura en el brazo' = she has a fracture in her arm. 'fractura abierta' = open fracture. Requires X-ray and often a cast.
Bandage — 'cambiar el vendaje de la herida' = to change the wound dressing. From 'venda' (bandage strip). 'vendaje compresivo' = compression bandage.
words.imageQuery: medical bandage dressing wound first aid

words.hint: Plaster cast — 'llevar el brazo en yeso' = to have the arm in a cast. Also means plaster/gypsum (building material). 'firmar el yeso' = to sign someone's cast.
words.imageQuery: plaster cast arm broken bone recovery

words.hint: Crutch — 'andar con muletas' = to walk on crutches. Used after leg injury or surgery. 'muleta' also refers to the red cape in bullfighting.
Wheelchair — 'ir en silla de ruedas' = to use a wheelchair. 'silla de ruedas eléctrica' = electric wheelchair. Essential mobility aid.
Ambulance — 'llamar a una ambulancia' = to call an ambulance. 'los paramédicos llegaron en ambulancia' = the paramedics arrived by ambulance.
Emergency room — 'ir a urgencias' = to go to the emergency room. Always plural in Spanish. 'sala de urgencias' = emergency ward. For life-threatening situations.
words.imageQuery: emergency room hospital sign urgent care

words.hint: Waiting room — 'esperar en la sala de espera' = to wait in the waiting room. Universal experience at clinics, hospitals, and doctors' offices.
Consultation — 'tener una consulta con el médico' = to have a consultation with the doctor. Also refers to the doctor's office/surgery itself.
Medical history — 'revisar la historia clínica del paciente' = to review the patient's medical history. Also 'expediente médico'. Contains all past treatments and diagnoses.
words.imageQuery: medical records history chart patient file

words.hint: Blood test — 'hacerse un análisis de sangre' = to have a blood test. 'los resultados del análisis' = the test results. Usually requires fasting beforehand.
words.imageQuery: blood test laboratory tube medical analysis

words.hint: X-ray — 'hacerse una radiografía / unos rayos X' = to have an X-ray. 'el técnico de rayos X' = the X-ray technician. Detects fractures and lung conditions.
Ultrasound — 'la ecografía del bebé' = the baby's ultrasound. Uses sound waves to create images of internal organs. 'ecografía abdominal' = abdominal ultrasound.
Biopsy — 'realizar una biopsia para analizar el tejido' = to perform a biopsy to analyze the tissue. Removes a sample of tissue to test for cancer or disease.
Anesthesia — 'anestesia general/local' = general/local anesthesia. 'el anestesiólogo administra la anestesia' = the anesthesiologist administers the anesthesia.
Suture/stitch — 'necesita puntos / suturas' = she needs stitches. 'retirar las suturas' = to remove the stitches. Closes a wound after injury or surgery.
Rehabilitation — 'la rehabilitación después de la cirugía' = rehabilitation after surgery. Also for addiction recovery ('rehabilitación de drogas').
Physiotherapy — 'ir a fisioterapia para recuperarse' = to go to physiotherapy to recover. Treats injuries and pain through physical exercises and manual therapy.

Psychologist — 'ir al psicólogo para hablar de tus problemas' = to go to the psychologist to talk about your problems. Feminine: 'la psicóloga'. Different from 'psiquiatra' (who can prescribe medication).
Nutritionist — 'el nutricionista hizo un plan de alimentación' = the nutritionist made a meal plan. Advises on diet and nutrition for health.
Ophthalmologist — 'el oftalmólogo revisó mi visión' = the eye doctor checked my vision. Don't confuse with 'optometrista' (optometrist) or 'óptico' (optician).
words.imageQuery: eye doctor ophthalmologist examination vision test

words.hint: Dentist — 'ir al dentista cada seis meses' = to go to the dentist every six months. 'la consulta del dentista' = the dentist's office. Feminine: 'la dentista'.
Cardiologist — 'el cardiólogo especialista en enfermedades del corazón' = the cardiologist, specialist in heart diseases. From 'cardio' (heart).
Pediatrician — 'el pediatra atiende a los niños' = the pediatrician treats children. Feminine: 'la pediatra'. From Greek 'paidós' (child).
Gynecologist — 'visita al ginecólogo para revisión anual' = annual visit to the gynecologist. Specialist in women's reproductive health. Feminine: 'la ginecóloga'.
Surgeon — 'el cirujano realizó la operación' = the surgeon performed the operation. 'cirujano plástico' = plastic surgeon. Feminine: 'la cirujana'.
words.imageQuery: surgeon operating room medical team surgery

words.hint: Specialist — 'el médico me derivó a un especialista' = the doctor referred me to a specialist. Any doctor with advanced training in one field.
Health insurance — 'tener seguro médico privado' = to have private health insurance. 'la tarjeta del seguro' = insurance card. Essential for accessing healthcare.
Medical appointment — 'pedir una cita médica' = to make a medical appointment. 'tengo cita con el médico mañana' = I have a doctor's appointment tomorrow.
Blood donation — 'donar sangre salva vidas' = donating blood saves lives. 'hacerse donante de sangre' = to become a blood donor. Usually at a 'banco de sangre'.
